name: Jerome Powell
role: Outgoing Chair, Federal Reserve (term ends May 15, 2026)
bio: Jerome H. Powell served as Federal Reserve Chair from February 2018 to May 15, 2026. Appointed by Trump and reappointed by Biden, he navigated the COVID-era stimulus, the subsequent inflation surge, and the rate-tightening cycle. After legal action by the Trump administration, Powell announced he would remain on the Fed board as a governor after his chairmanship concluded.

quotes.text: I will remain on the board for a period of time to be determined. The legal actions taken against me have left me no choice but to stay.
I intend to remain on the Board of Governors after my term as Chair ends, in the interest of institutional continuity.
quotes.topic: Fed transition / staying on board after Warsh confirmation

quotes.text: Central bank independence is not a slogan, it is the institutional precondition for price stability across political cycles. I will continue serving on the Board of Governors to help maintain that continuity.
quotes.topic: Final press conference as Fed chair
